title: JavaScript API Deprecation Warnings
html:
JavaScript API Deprecation Warnings

Add to My Topics
Add selected topic and subtopicsAdd Topic & Subtopics with topic headAdd entire publication
Watch
Download PDF
Save selected topicSave selected topic and subtopicsSave entire publication
Share
Email LinkCopy Link
Feedback
  • Last UpdatedApr 16, 2026
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Starting with Uniface 10.4.03.040, the DSP runtime automatically detects the use of deprecated JavaScript API functions and properties and displays warning messages in the browser’s developer console.

Each deprecation warning identifies:

  • The deprecated JavaScript API
  • The recommended replacement API
  • The Uniface module context (webtrigger or weboperation), when applicable
  • The exact location in your DSP JavaScript file where the call originated

These warnings help you identify and update deprecated API functions and properties early, before support is removed in a future release.

Deprecation warnings are informational only and do not interrupt or affect application execution.

Key Benefits

JavaScript API deprecation warnings provide the following benefits:

  • Automatic detection of deprecated API usage at runtime
  • Clear guidance on recommended replacement APIs
  • Uniface module context identifying the calling webtrigger or weboperation
  • Actionable call stack that points directly to the DSP JavaScript file and line number
  • Non‑blocking behavior that preserves existing functionality
  • One warning per deprecated function per browser session to avoid console clutter

How Deprecation Warnings Work

When a deprecated JavaScript API is called in a DSP application, the runtime writes a warning message to the browser console. The content of the warning depends on where the call originates.

Calls from a Uniface webtrigger or weboperation

If the deprecated API is called from within a Uniface webtrigger or weboperation, the warning includes module context and a call stack.

For example, the browser console displays a message similar to the following:


Function uniface.createDSPInstance() is deprecated. Use uniface.createInstance() instead.
  Called from: webtrigger 'detail' on script container of field 'MYBUTTON.MYENTITY.NOMODEL'.
    at uniface.Field.detail (mycomponent.js:12:31)
    at …

The Called from line identifies:

  • The Uniface module type (webtrigger or weboperation) and its name

  • The object that owns the script, such as:

    • Script container of a field
    • Collection script container of an entity
    • Occurrence script container of an entity
    • Script container of a component

The call stack that follows points directly to your DSP JavaScript file.

Calls outside a Uniface module

If the deprecated API is called outside a Uniface module (for example, from a custom widget or plain JavaScript), no module context is available.

In this case, the browser console displays a warning similar to the following:


Function uniface.getDSPInstances() is deprecated. Use uniface.getInstances() instead.
    at MyWidget.initialize (mywidget.js:45:18)
    at …
  • The call stack still identifies the exact location in your JavaScript source file where the deprecated API was invoked.

Viewing Deprecation Warnings

To view JavaScript API deprecation warnings:

  1. Open your DSP application in a supported web browser.
  2. Open the browser’s Developer Tools:
    • Chrome / Edge: Press F12, or right‑click and select Inspect
    • Firefox: Press F12, or right‑click and select Inspect Element
  3. Select the Console tab.
  4. Use your application as usual.
  5. Watch for deprecation warning messages in the console output.

You can click the file reference in the call stack (for example, mycomponent.js:12:31) to navigate directly to the line in your DSP JavaScript file where the deprecated API was called.

Note: Warnings appear only once per browser session for each deprecated function.

Deprecated JavaScript API Functions and Properties

The following JavaScript API functions and properties are deprecated. When used, they trigger a deprecation warning in the browser console.

Table 1. Deprecated Functions
Deprecated Function Recommended Alternative Description
uniface.createDSPInstance() uniface.createInstance() Creates a new component instance
uniface.getDSPInstance() uniface.getInstance() Retrieves an existing component instance
uniface.getDSPInstances() uniface.getInstances() Retrieves all component instances
uniface.getDSPInstanceNames() uniface.getInstanceNames() Retrieves the names of all component instances
Table 2. Deprecated Properties
Deprecated Property Recommended Alternative Description
uniface.DSPInstance uniface.Instance Component instance constructor
uniface.TYPE_DSPINSTANCE uniface.TYPE_INSTANCE Instance type constant

Migrating Deprecated Code

To migrate away from deprecated JavaScript APIs:

  1. Identify usage

    Run your application and check the browser console for deprecation warnings.

  2. Locate the code

    Click the file reference in the call stack to navigate directly to the deprecated call.

    If present, the Called from line also identifies the associated webtrigger or weboperation and owning object.

  3. Replace deprecated APIs

    Update your code using the recommended alternatives listed above.

  4. Test changes

    Verify that the application behaves as expected.

  5. Validate

    Confirm that deprecation warnings no longer appear in the browser console.

Migration Example

Before (deprecated):


var instance = uniface.createDSPInstance("MyComponent", "MyInstance");
var allInstances = uniface.getDSPInstances()

After (recommended):


var instance = uniface.createInstance("MyComponent", "MyInstance");
var allInstances = uniface.getInstances

Best Practices

  • Monitor the browser console during development and testing
  • Address deprecation warnings early to avoid future compatibility issues
  • Test applications thoroughly after replacing deprecated APIs
  • Keep code documentation aligned with current JavaScript API references
Was this topic helpful?
LikeDislike
